facts.claim: The European Union is treated as a unified regulatory rulebook for the purposes of the demo classification.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: Not legal advice; validate against current sector and member-state requirements

facts.claim: The United States and Canada are shown as a federated and sector-led operating pattern.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: Subnational and sector rules vary materially

facts.claim: The UAE and Saudi Arabia are shown as sector-specific localization markets.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: Requirements differ by regulator, workload and data class

facts.claim: Brazil is shown as a cross-border transfer procedure market.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: Sector and data-type exceptions require separate review

facts.claim: Türkiye is shown as a cross-border transfer procedure market.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: Validate current authority decisions and sector rules

facts.claim: Japan and South Korea are shown as cross-border transfer procedure markets.
facts.definition: Illustrative classification for demo and testing only
facts.caveat: The two markets are grouped only for visual demonstration
